canonical_definition: The degree to which a model's internal representations encode and utilize grammatical structure such as part-of-speech, dependency relations, and phrase types to inform processing decisions.
papers: How Do Large Vision-Language Models See Text in Image? Unveiling the Distinctive Role ofOCRHeads

definitions.text: The degree to which a model's internal representations encode and utilize grammatical structure such as part-of-speech, dependency relations, and phrase types to inform processing decisions.
The latent ability to represent and process grammatical structures such as agreement, word order, and sentence hierarchy across languages.
